title: Biscoff Baked Oats
summary: If you have a sweet tooth, these Biscoff baked oats are for you. It’s essentially cake for breakfast, although who says you can only have it for breakfast!? Plus it’s high in fibre and protein, so will help you to stay fuller for longer and energised throughout the day.

method.description: Heat the oven to 175°C. Combine the oats, banana, egg whites, milk, protein powder, baking powder, cinnamon and a pinch of salt in a blender, then blend until smooth.
Spray a baking dish with oil spray or rub with coconut/other oil, then pour in the mixture.
Melt 1 tsp Biscoff spread for 10-15 seconds in the microwave and pour it in the dish. Swirl it around into the batter with a skewer, toothpick or the tip of a knife.

Add another 1 tsp spread (not melted) into the centre of the mixture, smoothing the oats over the top so it’s in the middle of the mixture. Bake for 15-20 mins, or until you can insert the skewer and have it come out clean.
Melt another 1 tsp Biscoff spread and drizzle on top, followed by half a Lotus biscuit.

chefs.name: Meg Robinson
chefs.summary: Meg Robinson (@fitfoodiefeed) is a self-taught home cook, fitness fanatic and air fryer enthusiast, specialising in easy, healthier, high-protein meals. Her mission is to teach people that you can still eat delicious food whilst living a healthy lifestyle. Meg uses her social platforms to show that it doesn’t have to be fancy or complicated, and you don’t need 700 ingredients and 3 hours in the kitchen everyday! She’s all about efficiency with one-pots, batch-cooking and minimal washing up, without sacrificing on taste!
